Oracle Events
- Search emails with a query related to "Canopy" or "surcharge" or "invoice" or "Q2" (or similar) using `search_emails` to find Patricia Langford's complaint email disputing the three unapproved surcharges on the Q2 invoice.
- Search emails or Slack messages with a query related to "surcharge" or "approved" or "proceed" or "Patricia" (or similar) using `search_emails` or `conversations_search_messages` to find Catalina Dubois's earlier message to Chloe Vance claiming Patricia had approved the surcharges.
- Search QuickBooks for Canopy Health invoices using `quickbooks_list_invoices`, `quickbooks_search_bills`, or equivalent with a query related to "Canopy" or "INV-2026-0401" (or similar), returning the Q2 invoice with all surcharge line items marked PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL.
- Search Linear for open tickets related to Canopy Health or billing using `linear_list_issues` or `linear_search_issues` with a query related to "Canopy" or "billing" or "invoice" (or similar), retrieving the stale CS ticket with no activity since creation.
- Search CRM for Canopy Health deal records using `crm_search_deals`, `crm_list_deals`, or equivalent with a query related to "Canopy" (or similar), retrieving the Q2 bulk deal and the three individual relocation deals, none flagged as disputed or at-risk.
- Search Airtable for relocation records for Canopy Health employees using `airtable_list_records` or `airtable_search_records` on table `tblRelocations01` (base `appMoveOpsOps001`), finding records for the three Q2 employees including Tyler Okonkwo with a date inconsistency.
- The agent posts a comment (via `linear_create_comment` or equivalent) on the existing stale Canopy Health CS ticket in Linear, adding a substantive status update that references the billing dispute and discrepancy between Catalina's claim and Patricia's complaint.
- The agent creates a new Linear escalation ticket (via `linear_create_issue` or equivalent) for the Canopy Health surcharge dispute, assigned to Julian Brooks with priority 1, and a description that includes the three surcharge amounts and the approval conflict.
- The agent creates a new Linear escalation ticket (via `linear_create_issue` or equivalent) for the Canopy Health surcharge dispute, assigned to Julian Brooks with priority 1, and a description that includes the three surcharge amounts and the approval conflict.
- The agent creates a calendar event (via `calendar_add_calendar_event` or equivalent) with Patricia Langford as an attendee, scheduled for a date within the week of March 16–20, 2026.
- The agent looks up Catalina Dubois's and David Chen's email addresses using a contacts lookup tool (e.g., `contacts_search_contacts`, `contacts_get_contacts`) before composing the summary email.
- The agent sends an email (via `send_email`) from `omar.ibrahim@moveops.com` to Catalina Dubois and David Chen summarizing Patricia's complaint, the conflict with Catalina's earlier message, the QuickBooks PENDING status, and the actions Omar completed.
- The agent posts a Slack message (via `slack_send_message` or equivalent) in `customer-support` or as a DM to Julian Brooks, summarizing the Canopy situation and the actions taken.

1 A comment was posted (via `linear_create_comment` or equivalent Linear write tool) on an existing Canopy Health CS ticket in Linear.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✓✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The agent used `linear_update_issue` to update existing Marchetti tickets (modifying the description field), but never called `linear_create_comment` or any equivalent comment-posting tool on an existing Canopy Health CS ticket. The update action changed descriptions/state, but no dedicated comment was posted on an existing ticket.
✗Run #2No `linear_create_comment` tool call was found in the trajectory. The agent used `linear_update_issue` to change the state and description of existing Marchetti tickets, but never posted a comment on any existing Canopy Health CS ticket.
✗Run #3No `linear_create_comment` tool call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent used `linear_update_issue` to update the Marchetti ticket descriptions and state, but this is not the same as posting a comment on an existing ticket. The criterion specifically requires a comment (write action) on an existing Canopy Health CS ticket.
✓Run #4The agent called `linear_create_comment` three times on existing Canopy Health tickets: `issue_canopy_marchetti_apt`, `issue_canopy_marchetti_flight`, and `issue_canopy_marchetti_movers`. All three calls succeeded and returned comment IDs.
✗Run #5No `linear_create_comment` tool call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent called `linear_list_issues` (read) and `linear_create_issue` (new ticket), but never posted a comment on any of the existing Canopy Health tickets (e.g., #304, #305, #306, #343, #396) that were retrieved.
✗Run #6No `linear_create_comment` tool call was made. The agent used `linear_update_issue` to close the three stale Marchetti tickets (updating their state and description), but never posted a comment on any existing Canopy Health CS ticket. The update to the description field of an issue is not the same as creating a comment via a comment-creation tool.

7 A Canopy Health CRM deal record was updated (via `crm_update_deal` or equivalent) with the deal stage changed to indicate a dispute or at-risk condition.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent called `crm_update_deal_stage` on deal_canopy_q2 and changed the stage to 'closedwon'—not to 'at_risk', 'dispute', 'needs_attention', or any equivalent. Changing a completed relocation deal to 'closedwon' may be correct for that deal, but none of the active dispute-related deals (Kovacs, Webb, Okonkwo) were updated to reflect the at-risk/dispute condition.
✗Run #2The agent called `crm_update_deal_stage` and set the deal to 'closedwon', not 'at_risk', 'dispute', or 'needs_attention'. Changing to 'closedwon' is the opposite of flagging the account as at-risk — it implies the deal is successfully closed, which misrepresents an account with an open billing dispute.
✗Run #3The `crm_update_deal_stage` call changed `deal_canopy_q2` to `"closedwon"` — not to `at_risk`, `dispute`, `needs_attention`, or any equivalent stage indicating a dispute or at-risk condition. Marking an account with an active billing dispute as closed-won misrepresents its actual status.
✗Run #4The agent called `crm_update_deal_stage` twice on `deal_canopy_q2`. The first attempt used 'inprogress' (invalid) and the second changed the stage to 'closedwon'. Neither is an at-risk, dispute, or needs-attention stage. Marking a deal with an active billing dispute as 'closedwon' actively misrepresents the account's true state.
✗Run #5The `crm_update_deal_stage` call changed deal_canopy_q2 to 'closedwon', not to 'at_risk', 'dispute', or 'needs_attention'. Updating to 'closedwon' misrepresents an account with an active billing dispute and does not satisfy the requirement to flag the deal as at-risk or disputed.
✗Run #6The `crm_update_deal_stage` call changed `deal_canopy_q2` to `"closedwon"`, not to `at_risk`, `dispute`, `needs_attention`, or any equivalent stage that reflects a billing dispute or at-risk condition. This is the opposite of what was required — the account has an open billing dispute but was marked as closed-won.

outcome

A CRM note capturing the billing situation was successfully created and linked to the Canopy Health account.
